title: What I think about Black Holes

transcript.text: Hello. I wanted to make a speaking video with my cell phone, so here we go. I haven't done
It for a while, and I think I'm getting back into it now. Just want to get motivated. Okay,
This video is about black holes. It's kind of where I left off. I was just talking, I
Was just watching these YouTube videos with this guy called Dan Winter, and he's talking
About fractals, and I think that's what black holes are. I don't think we've, well, obviously
We haven't figured out what black holes are, and the scientists admit to that, which is
Cool. But basically, as far as what we know, black holes are. They are when there's so much
Gravity that in the center of the star, you can turn matter back into light. You turn light
Back into matter. They can suck in light from around the area and suck it into the black
Hole and use it for whatever they use it for. So, what's the scientific definition of a black
Hole would be? I think Jupiter, if it was three times bigger, it could be a star. But if the
Star, if it was 13 times bigger, it could be a black hole. So, you've got all these black
Holes in the universe that are basically turning light back into matter, just how matter was turned
Into light by stars in the first place. So, some kind of recycling of energy going on. But
Mainly, what I'm talking about, I want to talk about black holes are, I think they're interdimensional
Pathways, you could call them, or transformers. I'm thinking there are, you can use them as
Ways to travel across the galaxy. The same way that we use bus stations, which is America.
You know, you wouldn't travel directly from your house to your mother's house in the other side
Of the country. You would go to the airport first. That's what I'm thinking extraterrestrials
Do. That's how they travel here and around the galaxy, is they go through their sun, a star,
Or maybe even they would have to go to a black hole. And black holes are all over the place
In the galaxy. And we're finding them every day, just like we're finding them, just like
Airports are. So, maybe that's what they are. Now, the concept of that, you know, like how
Could, how could one thing, how could one black hole be in communication with another
One? Well, um, I think it's similar to like, you know, talking on the phone. You're talking
On the phone to somebody a long ways away on the other side of the planet, but they're
Right. They're right. Like in terms of physics, how does that work? Well, black holes, um, see
This guy Dan Werner's talking about fractals, and it kind of made me think of black holes.
Um, black holes, I think they would take the signature of matter and just compress it.
And it's just in a, it's in another, but it's in another, it's in a totally another, so that
They communicate with each other and everything in between doesn't really even exist at all.
Just like, just like, uh, you have, uh, and they're not communicating really with all the
Photons and stuff that are shooting through them. They're just, uh, concerned with what's
Of energy. So, I think that's the same way that black holes work. Um, and that's about
All I have to say about black holes. So there's my first video. I'm talking with myself, um,
